text: Designing a good REST API requires following several best practices. First, endpoints should be named with nouns, not verbs, like `/users` or `/orders`. Use standard HTTP status codes to communicate results: `200 OK` for success, `404 Not Found` when a resource doesn't exist, and `500 Internal Server Error` for server problems. Always include clear documentation that explains how to use each endpoint and what responses to expect. Finally, version your API using the URL path, for example `/v1/users`. This allows you to make changes without breaking existing applications that rely on your API. Following these practices makes your API easier to use and maintain.
